title: Stenandrium fasciculare
taxonomy: Stenandrium fasciculare is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae, commonly known as the acanthus family. This family is a group of flowering plants that are native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world. The Acanthaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies, with Stenandrium fasciculare belonging to the subfamily Acanthoideae. Stenandrium fasciculare can also be classified as part of the order Lamiales, which is a large and diverse order of flowering plants. The classification of Stenandrium fasciculare reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other plants in the Acanthaceae family and beyond.

intro: Stenandrium fasciculare, also known as 'Dwarf Stenandrium', is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities that may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues.
